steps.m: and all of you fear/be mindful of Him
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
Adding ل with kasra changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far). The alif is elongated with ٰ to show distance.
Adding ك makes this word point to something far away (like saying 'that over there')
Changing the ending to كُمْ shows this is addressing multiple people instead of just one person
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create comparative/superlative form. This pattern makes something 'more' or 'most'.
Added double-damma to show this is the subject and is indefinite. This word describes what is better/best for the people being addressed (لَّكُمْ = for you all).
Starting with the basic particle ل which shows direction or purpose
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and shows connection to the previous word
Adding كُمْ shows this is directed to multiple people being spoken to
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects 'if you know' to what came before it.
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Connected the letters and added vowels to make it a noun meaning the concept of being
Changed from the noun 'being' to a short command 'be!' by dropping the middle و letter. Like saying 'stop!' instead of 'stopping'
Added تُمْ to show this happened in the past and was done by multiple people being spoken to
Added vowels (fatha and kasra) to make it mean 'he knew' in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning from conditional 'if' to emphatic 'indeed'
Added ما to change the meaning from emphasis to restriction. This particle limits what comes after it, like saying 'you ONLY worship' instead of just 'you worship'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future instead of the past
Added تَ at the start to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing this action

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects what you worship (تَعْبُدُونَ) with besides/other than (دُونِ), showing that you worship things from besides Allah.
Connected the root letters that mean being lower or beneath something
Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'lower than' or 'beneath'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it. Together they mean 'besides/other than Allah'
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added ِ to show this connects to the words before it (مِن دُونِ meaning 'besides/other than'). The ٱ is just a different way of writing the initial alif.
Changed from single idol to multiple idols by adding أ at the start and ا after the ث

steps.m: idols (as the things being worshipped in تَعْبُدُونَ)
steps.n: Added ًا to show these idols are what is being worshipped in the sentence. This connects to the verb تَعْبُدُونَ (you worship).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's silent.
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action (you all)
Added و at beginning to connect this with the previous statement about worshipping idols
Changed first letter's vowel to kasra and added sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning the result of lying

steps.m: a falsehood (object of تَخْلُقُونَ - you fabricate)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being fabricated in the phrase 'you fabricate falsehood'
Starting with the basic particle form. The kasra (ِ) shows this is a particle that affects what comes after it.
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a basic particle to a strong emphasis particle. It makes the statement that follows it more certain and important.
A relative pronoun used to describe a group of people. Consists of the definite article ال + ذي (who/which) + plural suffix ين
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action, and added vowel marks to show it's happening now
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action together. This word appears in a statement saying what the people are doing - worshipping idols besides Allah.

steps.m: from/of (connects دُونِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: A preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects the word دُونِ (besides/instead of) to the idols being worshipped.
This root carries the basic idea of being lower, beneath, or inferior to something else
Adding the damma (ُ) changes it from a root into a common word meaning 'beneath' or 'besides'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word is connected to Allah (الله) right after it, showing it means 'besides Allah' or 'other than Allah'
Changed first letter to ل and added shadda to make it the proper name for God
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it combines with يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess) to mean 'they do not possess'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to ِ and end vowel to ُ
Added ون to show multiple subjects (the false gods mentioned in the ayah)
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
Root meaning 'provide' becomes the noun 'provision' by adding i-sound (kasra) and stopping mark (sukun)

steps.m: sustenance, provision (object of يَمْلِكُونَ - they possess)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this word is the object being possessed in the phrase 'they do not possess sustenance for you'

steps.m: to seek or strive for something

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels (fat-ha)
Added اِ at start and ت between first two letters to show extra effort in seeking
Changed ending to وا to show many people are being commanded

steps.m: so you all strive to seek (the sustenance from اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect this command with the previous statement about false gods not providing sustenance
Added vowels to make it into a word meaning location or presence. This word shows where to seek provision - near/with Allah.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one supreme deity

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to what comes after)
steps.n: Added a small line (kasra) under the last letter to show it belongs to or connects with what comes after
This root is about provision and sustenance - both giving and receiving it
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added ال (as ٱل) at start to make it specific, and doubled the ر with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: the provision/sustenance (object of يَمْلِكُونَ - they possess)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess)
Basic command form telling one person to worship
Added helper alif to make the command easier to pronounce
Changed from commanding one person to commanding a group
Added pronoun هُ to show who should be worshipped (referring to Allah)
Added و to connect this command with previous statements in the verse
Added vowels to make it a command meaning 'say thank you!'
Added helper alif to make pronunciation easier
Added وا to make the command plural - for many people

steps.m: and thank! (command to all of you)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and changed helper alif to ٱ
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Adding fatha to help with pronunciation
Adding هُ at the end shows this is 'for him'
Adding ۥٓ (an elongation mark) to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is just a spelling change.
Adding هِ makes it mean 'towards him'. This word shows where you all will be returned to in تُرْجَعُونَ (you will be returned).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone else did the action to the subject
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ at start, making it 'is returned' instead of 'was returned'
Added ونَ at the end to show this happens to multiple people
This is a compound particle made up of و (and) + إن (if)

steps.m: to deny or declare something false

steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense, showing strong or repeated denial
تُ shows it's either 'you' (male) or 'she' doing the action
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about denial. It connects this certainty to the previous word تُكَذِّبُوا۟ (you deny).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding a shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the meaning stronger, showing the denial was forceful or repeated
Added ة to create the word for a single community or nation that follows a leader or model
The singular word for 'nation' becomes plural by changing the middle of the word from مَّ to مَ and adding مٌ at the end. This word is doing the action of denying/rejecting in this ayah.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition that connects words, showing where something comes from or starts
Added shadda because this word comes before a word starting with ق. This is a pronunciation rule - when مِن comes before certain letters, the ن sound blends into the next letter.
Root letters ق-ب-ل got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the word meaning 'before'

steps.m: before you all (nations mentioned in أُمَمٌ)
steps.n: Added ِكُمْ at the end to show this refers to multiple people being addressed. The kasra (ِ) connects 'before' to 'you all'
Basic negative/interrogative particle that can mean 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' since it's negating what's required of the messenger.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates what is required of the messenger (الرَّسُولِ) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing responsibility or duty. In this ayah, it shows what is required of the messenger (ٱلرَّسُولِ).
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Added 'the' to make it specific and doubled the ر letter for pronunciation
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَى (upon) in the ayah
This is a basic particle that means 'except'. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْبَلٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (clear delivery/message) to say that the messenger's only duty is to deliver the clear message.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of conveying'

steps.m: the clear message (that must be conveyed)
steps.n: Adding ال and ُ makes it refer to the specific divine message that must be conveyed. In this ayah, it refers to the clear message that the Messenger must deliver.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'something/someone that makes clear' instead of 'made clear'

steps.m: the clear one (describing الْبَلٰغُ - the message)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to mean 'the' and ُ to show this describes 'the message' mentioned in the verse
This particle turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention to what follows
The و connects this question to what came before it, showing continuation of thought
لَمْ makes the verb يَرَوْا۟ (they see) negative and changes it to past tense. It's like saying 'did not see' instead of 'see'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Present tense form starts with يَ. The middle letters أَيَ combine into a single ى because they are weak letters that merge together
Added وْا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. The ى changes to و because of this ending.
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens. In this ayah, it's asking about how Allah begins creation.
Adding the vowel marks helps show how to pronounce this question word correctly
Adding the fatha at the end helps show this is a complete question word
Added vowels to connect the letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from regular verb to causative form (makes something happen). The hamza changed to ئ due to its position. This word shows Allah actively causing creation to begin.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

The word for 'a god' (إِلَه) becomes the unique name 'Allah' by adding ٱل and special merging. The shadda makes it stronger, showing this is the One True God. The ُ shows Allah is doing something in this sentence.

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to turn the action into a noun meaning 'the act or result of creating'. This word is what is being started/initiated by Allah in this ayah.

steps.m: the creation (object of يُبْدِئُ - he begins)
steps.n: Added the definite article and accusative case ending
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. The ُ (u-sound) and ّ (doubled m) make it stronger. It connects 'Do they not see?' with what comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Adds present tense marker يُ and changes internal vowels to show ongoing action. This word describes what Allah does with creation.

steps.m: He (Allah) repeats/returns it (referring to الْخَلْقَ - the creation)
steps.n: Adds pronoun suffix هُ meaning 'it' to show what is being returned (the creation). The ۥٓ marks are pronunciation helpers.

Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a basic word meaning 'if/that' into an emphasis word. It makes the statement stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English. Here it emphasizes that 'that is easy for Allah.'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)

steps.m: that (in this ayah, refers to the resurrection/bringing back to life mentioned before)
steps.n: The كَ is added at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun. This word points back to how Allah brings creation into being and then brings it back, showing that such an act is easy for Allah.
This is a helper word that shows something is on top of or based upon something else. Here it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) with 'easy' (يَسِيرٌ) to say 'that is easy upon Allah'
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness of Allah
Added kasra (i-sound) at the end to show Allah belongs to or owns something in this part of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and ٌ at the end to make it an adjective. This word describes 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) in the ayah, showing that something is easy for Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form. The middle letter و drops out because it's between two consonants in a command. This word is used to instruct Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to tell people something important.
Adding kasra changes this to show receiving the action
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects with 'the earth' to show where the traveling should happen.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic noun
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it follows the word فِى (in) in the ayah

steps.m: to look at or observe carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to a command meaning 'look!' by adding 'u' at start and changing the ending
Added فَ to show this command follows from something before it
Added وا to make the command for a group instead of one person
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable
Adding the final vowel mark to complete the word
This word is connected to يُنشِئُ (he produces) in this ayah, showing how Allah first began creation and then continues to reproduce it.

steps.m: to create or make something from nothing

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'creation'. This word is what was begun - it answers 'what did He begin?'
Added ال to make it specific and added a fatḥa at the end to show it's being acted upon
This is the basic form of the connecting particle that shows one action follows another after a delay
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects 'then look' with what comes after it, showing these are separate but connected actions.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'
Changed to the unique name of The One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ prefix and adjusting vowels

steps.m: he brings into being (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Changes from 'he creates' to 'he brings into being' by adding a damma (u sound) at the end to show this is the main action happening. The hamza (ئ) appears because of spelling rules.
Root letters were joined together to form a noun meaning 'growth/emergence'
Added ة to show one specific instance of creation
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda

steps.m: the creation (object of يُنشِئُ - He creates)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being created

steps.m: last, coming after (describes someone/something that comes after)
steps.n: Changed root letters into a word that means 'last' or 'coming after' by adding an alif and vowel marks
Added ة to turn 'last/final' into 'the afterlife' - like turning an adjective into a special noun

steps.m: the final life, the Hereafter (contrasts with الدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: Added ال to specify this refers to THE afterlife, and fatha at end shows this is the object of يُنشِئُ (creates/brings forth) in this ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizing what follows: اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the word, making it emphasize what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'Allah is capable of all things.'
Changed to the unique form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word and a shadda on the ل
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon by إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition that shows one thing is on top of or has power over another thing
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation helper to show the long 'aa' sound clearly. This word connects to كُلِّ (all) in this ayah to show power over everything.
Added damma and shadda to create the word meaning 'every/all'
Added kasra at the end because this word is describing شَىْءٍ (thing) in the phrase 'every thing'
Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'thing'. The hamza at the end is written separately because it comes after a sukun.
Added tanwin kasra at the end because this word is owned by كُلِّ (every/all) in the phrase 'every thing'. Also, the ي is written as ى when it has a sukun.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he had power) to an adjective meaning very powerful by adding ي and changing middle vowel - this is a common pattern for strong qualities
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is describing Allah's attribute of complete power over everything mentioned in the ayah
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intense action. This makes the punishment stronger or repeated.
Added يُ at the start to show it's happening now or in the future. The ُ at the end shows this is the main verb. This word is connected to يَشَآءُ (he wills) in the ayah.

This is a special word that asks about or refers to people. Here it shows who receives the action of punishment - meaning 'whoever He wills to punish.' It's connected to the verb يُعَذِّبُ (he punishes) and يَشَآءُ (he wills).

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added fatha marks and the weak letter ا replaced ي to make it easier to say

steps.m: he wants/wishes (subject of يُعَذِّبُ - he punishes)
steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now. This word describes what 'he' (Allah) does, connected to the word يُعَذِّبُ (he punishes) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form

steps.m: he shows mercy (parallel to يُعَذِّبُ - he punishes)
steps.n: Changed to present tense form. This verb pairs with يُعَذِّبُ (he punishes) in the ayah to show Allah's complete power over mercy and punishment

steps.m: and he shows mercy (paired with يُعَذِّبُ - he punishes)
steps.n: Added و at start to mean 'and'
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to the one who Allah wills to punish or show mercy to.
Added fatha vowels and combined the weak letter ي with hamza to form ا
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action. This word describes what the same 'he' does in يُعَذِّبُ (he punishes).
Changed the alif to alif madd (آ) for proper pronunciation
This is the basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's one of the common Arabic words that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: and to him (connected to تُقْلَبُونَ - you will be returned)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show who is being referred to (him). This connects to the next word تُقْلَبُونَ which means you will all be returned.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he turned' to 'is being turned' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show something is receiving the action

steps.m: you all are being turned over (back to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added ون to show this refers to multiple people. In this ayah, it's talking about everyone being returned to Allah.
The basic word مَا can mean either 'not' (negation) or 'what' (question word) depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's being used with بِمُعْجِزِينَ to say 'not able to escape'.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
This is the basic form of the second person pronoun used when speaking to one person
Adding م at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action to others
Added ين to make it plural

steps.m: by/with those who make others powerless (describing how they are not able to escape Allah's plan)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how/by what means, and final fatha for grammar
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to 'the earth' to show where something is located.
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it a proper noun
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it comes after the word فِي (in/on)
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'no'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it shows location within the earth.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The verb 'to rise up' becomes a noun 'sky' by adding ء at the end, since the sky is high up above us
Added ال with shadda on س to show this is a specific sky/heaven
Added kasra to show this word comes after the preposition فِى (in)

Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This word can mean either 'what' (asking about something) or 'not' (negating something) depending on context. In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's negating having protectors besides Allah.

steps.m: and not (connected to وَلَا in this ayah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This وَمَا works together with وَلَا later in the ayah to say 'and not... and not...'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added كُم at the end to show this is directed at multiple people. This word connects with the previous word بِمُعْجِزِينَ (those who can frustrate/escape) to show who cannot escape.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
The dot under the م (meem) disappears and a shadda is added on top to show it blends with the previous word's ending

steps.m: to be lower or beneath something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower than'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Changed to the unique name Allah by adding ال and a shadda, and changing the first letter. This special word is only used for the one true God in Islam.
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or relates to something else in the sentence
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it works with دُونِ to mean 'besides' or 'other than' when talking about Allah.

steps.m: to protect/be close to/take care of

steps.n: Basic past tense verb form showing someone did this action
Changed from a past-tense verb (he protected) to a noun meaning someone who does this action strongly - like changing 'protect' to 'protector'

steps.m: any strong protector (in context: besides Allah)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is referring to any protector, appearing in a phrase that denies having any protector besides Allah
This is a basic negation word that says 'no' or 'not' to something

steps.m: and not (connected to مُعْجِزِينَ - those who can escape)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous phrase. This shows that you cannot escape on earth AND cannot escape in heaven.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from verb 'helped' to noun meaning 'one who helps a lot'

steps.m: any helper/supporter (in relation to مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ - besides Allah)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is any general helper, coming after the words 'besides Allah' in the ayah
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding و at the beginning connects this to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: to reject, cover up, or deny truth

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This word is connected to يَئِسُوا۟ (they despaired) later in the verse, showing what these people did.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or demonstrate

steps.n: Root letters combine and change to make the word for 'sign' or 'miracle'
Changes the singular word into plural form
Adds preposition ب to show how something is done
Added kasra at the end because it connects to the next word (Allah) to show 'signs of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال with special pronunciation, doubling the ل with shadda, and adding kasra at the end to show connection to what follows
Changed the verb 'meet' into a noun 'meeting' by adding special vowels and letters
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to something after it
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added 'his' at the end to show this refers to meeting with Allah, mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'

steps.m: and those (referring to those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with the previous description of the disbelievers rejecting Allah's signs. This connection shows the consequence of their disbelief - they have despaired of Allah's mercy.

steps.m: to stand/rise up (basic meaning that led to 'people' as those who stand together)

Added kasra at the end because this word comes after مِن (from) and before اللَّهِ (Allah). Together مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ means 'instead of/besides Allah'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added kasra at end to show possession

steps.m: to be close to or protect
steps.n: Basic root meaning implies closeness or authority
Changed root letters into 'protector' by adding vowels (like changing 'protect' to 'protector')
Added shadda to intensify meaning
Changed to plural form. This word is what was taken (object) in the phrase 'those who took protectors besides Allah'

steps.m: to be like something or compare things
steps.n: Root conveys the idea of similarity or comparison
Added fathas to make it into a noun meaning 'an example' that we can use to explain things

steps.m: like the example (of العَنكَبُوتِ - the spider)
steps.n: Added كَ at start to mean 'like' and ِ at end to connect it to what comes after (the spider)
This is a quadrilateral root specifically for spider
Adds vowel marks to make it a proper noun meaning spider
Added the feminine ending pattern ُوت which is used for some animals and insects
Added the definite article to specify this particular spider

steps.m: the spider (possessed by/connected to something else)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this spider belongs to or is connected to something else in the sentence
Basic meaning of taking or grasping something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added ت and shadda to show doing something for yourself, like taking something as your own
Added ت at the end to show a female did the action
Adding sukun shows the action is complete/finished
Root related to dwelling and shelter
Basic noun pattern with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter. This word is what was taken (object) in the phrase 'the spider took a house'.
Added tanwin alif to show this is any house (not a specific one) and that it's being acted upon. The spider is taking any house, not a particular one.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain or important
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Root meaning relates to weakness
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: weakest (describing بَيْتُ ٱلْعَنكَبُوتِ - house of the spider)
steps.n: Added أ at start to change from 'became weak' to 'made weak/weakest'. Like changing from 'weak' to 'weakest' in English. Describes spider's house as weakest of all houses.
Root related to dwelling and shelter
Added vowel marks to form basic noun for a single house
Changed middle vowel to u-sound and added و to make it mean many houses
Added ال to make it definite (specific houses)
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to 'the spider' in the verse
Root related to dwelling and shelter
Changed verb into noun by adding fatha-sukun pattern. This word is the subject of أَوْهَنَ (weakest) in the phrase 'surely the house of the spider is the weakest.'
Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis (meaning 'surely/indeed'). Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action.
This is a noun with no clear root - it's the word for spider
Adds basic vowel marks to make the word pronounceable
Added 'وت' at the end to make the complete word for spider
Added the definite article 'the' and kasra at the end because it's the subject of 'took' (ٱتَّخَذَتْ) in this ayah
This is a conditional word that shows something that could have happened but didn't. It connects to يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) to mean 'if they had known' - showing they didn't actually know.
Added fatha marks to show it happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of word.

steps.m: they were (subject of يَعْلَمُونَ - they know)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) instead of just one person (he). This word connects to يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in the next part.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future. The ْ (sukun) on ع shows it has no vowel, and the ُ (damma) on ل shows it's present tense.
Added ون to show multiple people (specifically men or mixed group) are doing the action
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that introduces and connects statements
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the emphasis stronger. This particle emphasizes that Allah definitely knows what they call upon.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

steps.m: Allah (the one true God, object of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, doubled the middle letter to show uniqueness and supremacy, and added fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb يَعْلَمُ (He knows)
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make a past tense verb

steps.m: he knows (subject of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ - Indeed, Allah)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject doing the action. This word describes what Allah does in the phrase 'Indeed, Allah knows'

steps.m: that which (object of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: This word connects to يَعْلَمُ (He knows) and introduces what Allah knows - everything they call upon besides Him

steps.m: to call upon, invite, or pray to

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and form past tense. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at start to show present tense. Changed vowels to show ongoing action. This verb describes what Allah knows about (from يَعْلَمُ - He knows).
This is a basic particle that shows separation or distance from something. In this ayah, it's used with دُونِهِۦ (besides Him) to show that the false gods are separate from or besides Allah.

steps.m: to be below or besides something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it from a basic root to a preposition meaning 'below' or 'besides'
Added kasra (ِ) because it's being possessed by what comes after it
Added هِۦ to show it refers to Allah (Him)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it connects دُونِهِۦ (besides Him) to what comes after it.
Root letters ش-ي-أ become the common noun شَيْء meaning 'thing' or 'something'
Added kasratain (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite and in genitive case

steps.m: a thing (that they call upon besides Allah)
steps.n: The words are actually the same - just written slightly differently. Both have tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end showing it's affected by the word مِن (from/of) before it.
Basic pronoun meaning 'he'
Added diacritics to show correct pronunciation: damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects 'he' to the following words ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (the Mighty, the Wise)
Adding ي in the middle and the vowels makes this describe someone or something that has a lot of strength or power
Adding ال makes it specific (like 'the' in English). The ُ shows this is describing Allah in this ayah. This is one of Allah's names, showing His perfect and complete might and power.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding ي between second and third root letters with kasra creates an intensive adjective meaning 'very wise'
Adding ال makes it definite (the), and ُ shows it's a subject. This is one of Allah's Divine names describing His perfect wisdom.
This is a demonstrative word used to point to something far away

steps.m: and that (feminine, pointing to those examples)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word is pointing to الْأَمْثٰلُ (the examples) that follow.
Added vowel marks to make the basic singular noun form
This is the plural form of مَثَل (example/parable)
Added 'the' at the beginning and ُ at the end because this word is doing the action in نَضْرِبُهَا (we strike/coin them)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: we present them (the examples mentioned in الْأَمْثٰلُ)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this refers to presenting the examples (الْأَمْثٰلُ) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: Root means being social/friendly, turned into the word for people/humans
Added ال (the) at the start to mean 'the people' instead of just 'people'

steps.m: for the people (who are being given examples in وَتِلْكَ ٱلْأَمْثٰلُ نَضْرِبُهَا)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show these are the people FOR whom the examples are given, and added ِ at the end because it comes after لِ
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَعْقِلُ (understand/comprehend) in this ayah
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This connects to the previous statement about giving examples to people.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word describes who understands the examples (أَمْثٰلُ) that are being given.

steps.m: he understands them (referring to الْأَمْثٰلُ/the examples)
steps.n: Added هَآ to show what is being understood - referring back to the examples (الْأَمْثٰلُ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is the basic negative particle that forms part of the exception word
Adding shadda emphasizes the 'laam' sound

steps.m: except (in the ayah: and none comprehends them except the learned)
steps.n: Adding إِ at the beginning changes the meaning from negation to exception. In this ayah, it shows that only the learned people (العٰلِمُونَ) understand the examples.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes from a past tense verb (he knew) to a noun meaning 'someone who knows/has knowledge'. Like changing 'knew' to 'knower'.
Makes the singular noun عالم into a plural noun for masculine entities
Added 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the scholars' instead of just 'scholars'

steps.m: to create or bring something into existence from nothing

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what Allah did - He is mentioned right after this word in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Adding 'al' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity
Adding damma shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Root meaning 'rise up' became noun 'sky' by adding alif and hamza at end
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Added kasra at end to show it's the object of خَلَقَ (He created). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this style.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. This word is what Allah created (object of خَلَقَ - He created).
Added ال to specify this particular earth
Added و to connect this with السماوات (the heavens) mentioned before it
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being created
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show emphasis. This word relates to how Allah created everything.
Definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: with the truth (describing how Allah created)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how Allah did the creation mentioned in خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ (Allah created)

steps.m: with the truth (describing how Allah created the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the word بِ. This whole phrase explains how Allah created the heavens and earth - He did it with truth and purpose.

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah truly/definitely created the heavens and earth. It's like adding an exclamation mark or saying 'definitely' in English.
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) with 'a sign' (لَءَايَةً), showing that something exists within or as part of something else.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) to show distance
Added ل with kasra as a connector for the suffix that will come next

steps.m: that (referring to Allah's creation of the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is pointing to something specific that was just mentioned (the creation of the heavens and earth)
Forms a noun meaning 'sign' by combining the root letters with a long alif and adding ة at the end
Adds fatha at the end to show this word is being used as an object
Adds 'la' to emphasize that this is definitely/truly a sign
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Added ين to make it plural, showing we're talking about many believers
Added لِلْ which combines 'for' (لِ) and 'the' (ال)
Added shadda on ل and fatha at the end for proper pronunciation
Added sukun on first letter and damma at end to make basic command form

steps.m: Recite! (emphatic command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ٱ at start to make it an emphatic command. This word starts verse 29:45 where Allah commands Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to recite what has been revealed to him.
Added fatha on م to show this is a relative pronoun. In this ayah, it connects to the verb اتْلُ (recite), showing what should be recited.
Added alif with madda (آ) instead of regular alif (ا) to show lengthened pronunciation. This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show the revelation was received. The word connects to إِلَيْكَ which shows who received the revelation.
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. This word has no root because it's a preposition.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix
The كَ suffix shows this is directed 'to you'. This word is receiving the action of أُوحِىَ (was revealed) in this ayah.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is one of the basic Arabic prepositions meaning 'from'
Added fatha (َ) at the end because it connects to the next word الْكِتٰبِ (the Book). This helps make the pronunciation flow better between words.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from action 'wrote' to object 'book' by changing middle vowel to long ā. Like changing 'wrote' to 'writing'.
Adding 'Al' (ال) at the start makes it 'the book' instead of just 'book'
The kasra at the end shows this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Changed to a command form telling one person to establish something. The و disappeared because it's a weak letter.
Added 'and' to connect with previous command (recite/read) in the verse. This command specifically refers to establishing prayer (الصَّلَوٰةَ) which comes right after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed from 'praying' action to 'prayer' thing by adding ٰة at the end
Makes the word specific by adding 'the' to it
Adding fatha at end to show this is the object of the command 'establish' (أَقِمِ)
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ن changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. This helps show that what comes next is definitely true.
Basic root meaning showing the concept of prayer or worship
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
The weak letter و changes to ة and alif is added to create a noun for a single instance of prayer
Added ال to make it specific. The ل combines with ص to make it doubled (shadda)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the command 'establish' (أَقِمِ). The alif is written as a small alif above the و.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: she prevents/forbids (referring to الصَّلَوٰةَ - the prayer)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to make it 'she/it' in present tense (referring to prayer as the doer). The first vowel changed to a silent mark (sukun).

steps.m: she prevents/forbids (referring to الصَّلَوٰةَ - the prayer)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the final ى to show it's a long vowel sound.
This is a basic preposition showing separation or topic. In this ayah, it shows what the prayer keeps us away from (الْفَحْشَآءِ - immorality).
The sukun (ْ) changes to kasra (ِ) because the next word starts with ال. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a basic noun meaning
Added اء at the end to intensify the meaning and make it into an abstract concept
Added ال to make it specific. This word is being prevented/forbidden by prayer (تَنْهَىٰ)

steps.m: from the great obscenity/indecency (object of تَنْهَىٰ عَنِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after عَنِ (from/against). Prayer prevents/forbids from this.
This root carries the basic meaning of rejecting or disapproving of something
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix and vowel pattern changes this from an action to a thing that is disapproved of
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to عَنِ (away from) in the ayah
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific evil deed rather than any evil deed

steps.m: and the evil deed (object of تَنْهَىٰ عَنِ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous word ٱلْفَحْشَآءِ (shameful deeds). Both are things that prayer keeps us away from.
Basic root meaning showing the concept of remembering or mentioning something
Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning the act itself
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in أَكْبَرُ (is greater)
Added لَ to emphasize the importance of remembrance
Added وَ to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped in this special word.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on lam for emphasis, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects to previous word
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative/superlative form. In this ayah, it's comparing the greatness of Allah's remembrance to other things.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. Unlike other Arabic words, it cannot be broken down into root letters.
Added shadda on ل to show it is pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (knowing) in this sentence

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a waṣla form since it connects with the و before it in pronunciation
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added يَ at beginning to show present/future tense and changed middle vowel to sukun
This is a particle that connects to the verb تَصْنَعُونَ (you do/make) to form the meaning 'what you do'. In this ayah, Allah is saying He knows what people do.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning

steps.m: you all make/create (in the ayah: what you all do/make)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed
Starting with the basic negation particle لا which means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This وَلَا is used to negate the verb تُجٰدِلُوٓا۟ (argue) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ا shows this is an argument between two people (back and forth)
تُ at start shows this is 'you' or 'she' doing the action

steps.m: you (all) argue with someone (negative command in وَلَا تُجٰدِلُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Changed from single person to many people by adding وا, and made it into a negative command (don't argue) by changing the vowels

steps.m: to dwell, inhabit, belong to a family

steps.n: Root letters put together with fatha-sukun pattern to make a simple noun meaning 'people/family'

steps.m: people, family, inhabitants (object of تُجٰدِلُوا - argue with)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تُجٰدِلُوا (argue with)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The verb 'he wrote' changes to mean the thing that is written (a book). Like how drawing makes a drawing, writing makes a book. The word كتاب appears after أهل (people) to mean 'people of the book'.
Adding ال at the start makes it specific - 'the book' instead of just any book
The kasra ending (ِ) shows this word is possessed by or connected to something else. In this ayah, it's possessed by أهل (people), forming 'people of the book'

steps.m: except (those who wronged from أَهْلَ ٱلْكِتٰبِ)
steps.n: This is a basic word that creates an exception. In this ayah, it excludes 'those who wronged' from the previous command about how to debate with the People of the Book.
Added ى to make it feminine. This is the basic demonstrative pronoun.
Added ٱلَّ to change it from a demonstrative to a relative pronoun (meaning 'that which' instead of just 'that')
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something should be done. In this ayah, it shows how to debate with the People of the Book - with/by that which is best.
This is a feminine pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before (in this case, it refers to the way or manner mentioned as ٱلَّتِى). It's the subject of the word أَحْسَنُ (better/best).
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the start to make it mean 'more/better than', like turning 'good' into 'better'
This is a fundamental exceptive particle in Arabic that creates an exception to what came before it. In this ayah, it's saying not to argue with the People of the Book EXCEPT in the best way.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it more specific, changing from just 'who' to 'those who'

steps.m: those who (referring to some of the People of the Book)
steps.n: Final spelling adjustments: changing ال to ٱلّ with shadda, and adding fatha at the end. In this ayah, it refers to some of the People of the Book mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to do wrong or harm to someone/something
he did wrong (subject of إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb. This word refers back to those mentioned in إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ (except those who)

steps.m: they did wrong (subject of إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This refers to a group of people from the People of the Book mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: from them (referring to those who wronged/did injustice)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows this refers to 'them' - specifically referring back to الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ (those who did wrong) mentioned earlier in the verse
The وا makes it for a group of people
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Added shadda to emphasize the strength of belief
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word comes after قُولُوا (say), showing what we should say.
This is a demonstrative word that points to something or someone. Since it doesn't come from a root, we start with its basic form.
Adding ال (the) turns 'who/which' into 'the one who/which', making it more specific

steps.m: in that which (connects to أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: The preposition بِ shows connection. The alif in ال becomes ٱ (alif wasl) after a preposition. The long vowel mark ٓ shows continuation of sound.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes someone cause the action instead of doing it directly - like changing 'come down' to 'make come down' or 'send down'
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it - like changing 'he sent down' to 'it was sent down'
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix
Adding نا shows this is happening to 'us'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at start makes it causative - someone is making something happen
Changed vowels to make it passive - showing something was done to the subject rather than the subject doing it
Added و at start to mean 'and', connecting this to the previous statement
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to you all (receiving what was sent down أُنزِلَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to what was sent down to the people being addressed.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding alif after laam, making it mean 'something that is worshipped'
The alif is written as a small mark above to show it's a long 'aa' sound
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added نا at the end to show possession (our)
Added و at beginning to connect this with previous statement

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ُ at the end to show this is doing something (like 'god' in 'god created')
Added كُمْ meaning 'your' when speaking to multiple people
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and'
Added short vowels (fatha and kasra) to make it mean 'he became one' in the past tense
Changes from verb 'became one' to noun meaning 'one'
Adds tanween dammah to make it 'a/one' instead of 'the one'
This is a pronoun meaning 'we'. The ُ at the end shows it's doing something in the sentence.

steps.m: and we (connected to إِلٰهُنَا - our God)
steps.n: Adding وَ (and) connects this with the previous statement about 'our God'. It shows these ideas are related.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ at the end to show this is 'to him' or 'for him'
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ون to make it plural, showing multiple people who submit
Added لِ and كَ to the demonstrative pronoun ذا to make it refer to something far away ('that' instead of 'this')
Added كَ at beginning to mean 'like/similar to'
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what follows (We sent down)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action causative - someone making something else descend
Adding نَا shows 'we' did the action. The long alif at the end (آ) is for emphasis. This word connects to إِلَيْكَ (to you) in the ayah.

steps.m: to/towards (preposition for أَنزَلْنَآ - We sent down)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing direction or destination. It connects with the verb أَنزَلْنَآ (We sent down) to show where/to whom something was sent.

Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word نِعْمَة (favor) before it. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting form (ٱ). The middle lam has a shadda to show it's doubled.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb
Changes كَفَرَ (past) to يَكْفُرُ (present) by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who reject Allah's favors while believing in falsehood.
This is a special word used to refer to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
This word is part of a question asking 'who is more unjust than the one who...' showing it's comparing levels of injustice
This is the basic question word meaning 'who'
Added مِن before the word to show something comes from or is compared to something else
The ن of مِن combines with the م of مَن to make a doubled م (shown by the ّ mark). This happens because these sounds are similar and it's easier to say them together.
Added ِ at the end to show this word is connected to the verb افْتَرَىٰ (he invented) that comes after it in the ayah
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added اِفْتَ to show someone made up or invented something that wasn't real before (like telling a lie)
Changed spelling of initial alif to match Uthmani script, and added alif maqsurah at end
This is a basic connecting word that shows something is on top of or against something else. Here it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show the lie is against Allah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the proper name of God with definite article ال and emphasis on ل. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence.
Adding vowel marks to show this is a complete past tense verb meaning 'he lied'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being fabricated in the phrase 'whoever fabricates a lie'

steps.m: or (connecting two parts: 'invents a lie' or 'denies the truth')
steps.n: Basic form of the conjunction particle that gives a choice between alternatives. In this ayah, it connects two types of wrongdoers: those who make up lies about Allah and those who deny the truth.

steps.m: or (connecting two parts: 'invents a lie' or 'denies the truth')
steps.n: Added fatha on أ and sukun on و to show correct pronunciation
Adding the vowels connects the letters and makes it a past tense verb

steps.m: he denied/rejected as false (the truth حَقّ)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter changes the meaning from simply lying to actively rejecting or denying something as false. In this ayah, it refers to rejecting the truth (حَقّ) when it came to them.
Root letters ح-ق-ق became the noun 'truth' by adding a shadda (double letter) and fatha marks
Added definite article to specify this particular truth
Added kasra because it follows the preposition بِ (with/by)
Added the preposition بِ to show how the truth is being used
Starting with the two separate particles لَمْ (not) and مَا (what/when) that combine to form this word
The two م letters merge together with a shadda. The meaning shifts to focus on the timing of an event that already happened.
Added alif with madda (آ) which is just a writing variation of اا - it doesn't change the meaning
Added هُ with extension marks (ۥٓ) to show this means 'to him'. This word comes after لَمَّا (when) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a complete word. The fatha on ل and sukun on ي show this is in past tense form, though it gives present meaning.
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a rhetorical question expecting 'yes' as an answer. In this ayah, it's asking 'isn't Hell a dwelling place for the disbelievers?'
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position
Added the ى which is the standard written form of this preposition. In this ayah, it shows location within Hell (جَهَنَّمَ).

steps.m: Hell (as the destination in جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى - Hell as a dwelling)
steps.n: This is a proper noun for the place of punishment in the afterlife. It doesn't come from an Arabic root because it's a borrowed word. In this ayah, it's connected to مَثْوًى (dwelling place) to show Hell is a dwelling for the disbelievers.
The shadda (ّ) on the ن shows emphasis, making the word stronger and more intense in meaning

steps.m: to stay or settle in a place

steps.n: Added vowels to make it into a basic past-tense verb
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens. The ى at the end shows it's a special type of noun.

steps.m: to reject, to cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Makes the word plural to show many people who reject
Makes it specific which rejecters we're talking about

steps.m: for the ones who reject (related to مَثْوًى/dwelling place)
steps.n: Shows this word is connected to مَثْوًى (dwelling place) in the ayah, indicating the dwelling place is for these people
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who' or 'which', used to describe someone or something
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ال at the beginning makes it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'
Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: to put in effort, to work hard
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of striving or putting in effort
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif after first letter to show increased effort or doing something with someone else
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together
This is a preposition meaning 'in' that shows where the striving (جٰهَدُوا) happens
Added نا which means 'us', showing who is being strived in. This connects to the verb جٰهَدُوا (they strived) to show they strived in our cause/way.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The ن at the start shows 'we' are doing the action
Adding ل at the beginning transforms it to future tense with emphasis

steps.m: surely we will guide (with strong emphasis)
steps.n: Adding نّ at the end adds strong emphasis
The هُمْ (hum) attached at the end means 'them' - it shows who is being guided
This root relates to paths and ways
Added damma marks to make the plural form meaning many paths

steps.m: our paths (object of لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ - we will guide them)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show these are our paths. This word is what they are being guided to in the phrase 'we will guide them to our paths'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. Here it emphasizes that 'Allah is with those who do good'

steps.m: and indeed/and certainly (emphasizes ٱللَّهَ لَمَعَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ)
steps.n: The و adds 'and' to the beginning
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added a fatha mark to show this word is in the accusative case
This means 'with' or 'along with'

steps.m: surely with (emphasizing that Allah is with المحسنين)
steps.n: The لَ adds emphasis to show certainty. This word connects to المحسنين (the doers of good) in this ayah, emphasizing that Allah is surely with them.

steps.m: to do good, to make beautiful
steps.n: This root is about goodness and beauty
Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make it mean 'became good/beautiful' in the past tense
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'one who' and changed the middle vowels to show someone who does good deeds
Added ين to show multiple people who do good
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: the ones who do good (after لَمَعَ)
steps.n: Added َ at the end because it comes after لَمَعَ (surely with) in the ayah
